customer_anxiety_profile: Jody is a research-primed, technically engaged homeowner who has already spoken to Dominion's solar program and received a specific output estimate (50% of needs). She has local code knowledge (4-foot setback, rubber membrane roof) and is actively stress-testing whether solar is viable for her specific situation. She is not hostile — she booked without friction — but she has unresolved feasibility doubts that the closer must address before selling.
unresolved_concerns: Whether solar can generate more than 50% of her needs given her roof footprint (Dominion told her 50% was the ceiling)
Whether the rubber membrane roof with 4-foot setback constraints makes the install viable at all
She has not yet moved into the home — no utility bill exists; design was scoped off square footage (1,330 sq ft, 2-story)
sensitive_topics_raised: Rubber membrane roof with local code 4-foot setback requirement — closer should verify this is not a hard DQ before the call
Customer is pre-move-in; no utility history at this address; first electricity use approximately one month from call date
exact_promises_rep_made: Phone call appointment confirmed for tomorrow afternoon at 2:00 PM
Text confirmation to be sent after the call
closer_should_know: The setter engaged Jody's panel-count and roof-feasibility questions directly and provided specific (self-admitted inaccurate) panel count estimates during the call. Jody may reference those numbers. The closer should not validate or argue the setter's arithmetic — instead, anchor to the satellite design as the authoritative source and use it to replace the setter's estimates cleanly. Jody's core question is 'will this actually work for my roof?' — answer that first before any savings conversation.
